summary: IFC-187 attestation falsely claimed 10 tRPC endpoints existed (7 actual). The 4 missing endpoints have now been implemented and tested.

issues_found.description: task.assign endpoint did not exist in task.router.ts despite attestation claiming it at line 395
task.reschedule endpoint did not exist despite attestation claiming it at line 426
issues_found.suggested_fix: Add reschedule procedure wiring to TaskService.updateDueDate

issues_found.description: task.getReminders endpoint did not exist despite attestation claiming it at line 446
issues_found.suggested_fix: Add getReminders procedure wiring to TaskService.getOverdueTasks + getTasksDueSoon

issues_found.description: task.getByEntity endpoint did not exist despite attestation claiming it at line 467
issues_found.suggested_fix: Add getByEntity procedure wiring to TaskService.getTasksByEntity

issues_found.description: Validator schemas (assignTaskSchema, rescheduleTaskSchema, getRemindersSchema, getByEntitySchema) did not exist
issues_found.suggested_fix: Add 4 Zod schemas for the new endpoints

changes_made.action: Added 4 new Zod schemas: assignTaskSchema, rescheduleTaskSchema, getRemindersSchema, getByEntitySchema
Added 4 new tRPC procedures: assign, reschedule, getReminders, getByEntity
Added 20 new tests for the 4 new endpoints (58 total, up from 38)

root_cause: False attestation — the IFC-187 attestation claimed all 10 endpoints existed with specific line numbers, but only 7 were actually implemented. The service layer (TaskService) had all necessary methods, but they were never wired to tRPC endpoints.
